title: Luna 10: First Moon Orbiter
story: Zoom! On April 2, 1966, a small spacecraft named Luna 10 made history. It became the first human-made object to orbit the Moon. Luna 10's success opened a new chapter in space exploration, paving the way for future missions to study the Moon up close.

title: Radar: The Invisible Eye in the Sky
story: Zap! Scottish physicist Robert Watson-Watt had a brilliant idea that would change the world. On April 2, 1935, he received a British patent for RADAR. This amazing invention uses radio waves to detect objects far away, even through clouds and darkness. It's like giving planes and ships a superpower to 'see' things they can't with regular eyes. Radar helps keep us safe in the air and on the sea.
activity_question: What does RADAR use to detect objects?
bonus_fact: RADAR stands for RAdio Detection And Ranging.

title: Antarctica's Surprising Swampy Past
story: Imagine lush forests where icy winds now howl! In 2020, scientists made a jaw-dropping discovery about Antarctica. They found evidence that parts of the frozen continent were once warm and swampy. This happened between 93 and 83 million years ago, during the age of dinosaurs. On April 2, researchers revealed that western Antarctica had been covered in temperate forests, much like those in New Zealand today.
activity_question: What kind of environment did scientists discover once existed in western Antarctica?
follow_up_question: What modern-day forests are similar to those found in ancient Antarctica?

bonus_fact: Lake Tulare was once the largest body of water west of the Mississippi River.

title: Lights, Camera, Action: America's First Movie Theater

It was the largest theater in Los Angeles
It was powered entirely by electricity

The trumpet. Their talent led to hit songs like 'Paper Doll' and 'Up A Lazy River', delighting listeners on April 2 and beyond.
activity_question: What was Herbert Mills' specialty in The Mills Brothers?
bonus_fact: The Mills Brothers could sound like a full band using only their voices!

title: Brushstrokes of Equality: Charles White's Art
story: Charles White picked up his first paintbrush and never looked back. Born in Chicago on April 2, 1918, he grew into a talented artist who used his skills to shine a light on important issues. White's paintings, like 'The Contribution of the Negro to American Democracy,' showed the world the strength and beauty of African American culture.
activity_question: What famous painting did Charles White create?
activity_choices: The Contribution of the Negro to American Democracy
activity_answer: The Contribution of the Negro to American Democracy
follow_up_question: How do you think art can help people understand important issues in society?

title: A Scrawl-ing Success Story
story: In a world of blank pages, Mark Shulman picked up his pen and began to write. Born in Rochester, New York on April 2, 1962, Shulman would grow up to become a beloved American children's author. His book 'Scrawl' captured the hearts of young readers, proving that even the messiest handwriting can tell the most captivating stories.
activity_question: What is the title of one of Mark Shulman's books mentioned in the story?
follow_up_question: Have you ever tried writing your own story? What would it be about?

title: Reggae Star Garnett Silk's Musical Journey
story: Garnett Silk, born Garnet Smith on April 2, 1966, in Manchester, Jamaica, grew up to become a beloved reggae singer and songwriter. His soulful voice and powerful lyrics touched hearts worldwide. Silk's hit songs like 'Hey Mama Africa' and 'Greenline' showcased his talent and passion for music.
activity_question: What was Garnett Silk's birth name?
bonus_fact: Reggae music originated in Jamaica in the late 1960s, blending elements of ska and rocksteady.

title: Swinging to Success: Ayako Okamoto's Golf Journey
story: Ayako Okamoto swung her way into golfing history! Born in Hiroshima, Japan in 1951, she became a powerhouse on the golf course. Okamoto's talent shone brightly as she clinched an impressive 17 LPGA Tour titles. Her skills were on full display when she finished as the runner-up in the prestigious US Open tournament in 1987. April 2 marks the birthday of this remarkable Japanese golfer who left her mark on the sport.
activity_question: What major golf tournament did Ayako Okamoto finish as runner-up in 1987?
follow_up_question: How many LPGA Tour titles did Ayako Okamoto win in her career?

title: Italian Marathon Star Born
story: Gelindo Bordin raced into the world on April 2, 1959. Born in Vicenza, Italy, he grew up to become a legendary marathon runner. Bordin's incredible speed and endurance led him to win Olympic gold in 1988. His victory made Italy proud and inspired many young athletes to lace up their running shoes.
activity_question: What Olympic medal did Gelindo Bordin win?
follow_up_question: What sport did Gelindo Bordin excel in?

title: Sci-Fi Star Born: Joan D. Vinge
story: A new star of science fiction entered our world on April 2, 1948. Joan D. Vinge was born in Baltimore, Maryland, destined to become a celebrated author. Her imagination would soar, crafting tales of distant planets and futuristic worlds. Vinge's talent shone brightly, earning her two prestigious Hugo Awards for her captivating stories.
activity_question: What prestigious awards did Joan D. Vinge win for her science fiction writing?
bonus_fact: Joan D. Vinge also wrote a book in the famous Dune series, expanding that sci-fi universe.
